party_a.current_emotional_state: cautiously_optimistic — visible shift from defensive vigilance to conditional openness

emotional_arc.assessment: cautiously_optimistic — visible shift from defensive vigilance to conditional openness

accumulated_interests.interest: Child's emotional regulation and stability
Predictability in routine during school week
Having input/control over exchange timing and conditions
Being seen as the parent who ensures structure
Maintaining primary influence over child's school-week routine structure
Being validated as the parent who ensures stability
Having advance notice and veto capacity over schedule changes
Child's emotional regulation (genuine, but fused with own control needs)
Child's emotional regulation and stability during transitions
Explicit reliability markers that reduce unpredictability risk
Maintaining structural influence over school-week routines
Being acknowledged as the parent who ensures consistency
Having input into trial design parameters (not just accepting imposed change)
Minimizing school-week disruption during experimental phases

risk_signal_history.signal: Framing position as child-centered may obscure own control interests
Pre-emptive limitation framing ('should stay limited') before hearing Party B
Child welfare framing used exclusively — own interests not yet voiced as legitimate separate needs
Pre-emptive limitation language ('should stay limited') suggests gatekeeping pattern risk
Conditional framing ('if reliability conditions are explicit') may be used to add incrementally restrictive conditions if not bounded early
Has not yet acknowledged Party B's equal parental status concern — focused exclusively on logistics and child impact

relational_posture_progression.posture: defensive_anchor — holding ground on initial frame while waiting to see if Party B will challenge directly
conditionally_collaborative — first signal of flexibility represents genuine movement but conditions are protective scaffolding, not obstruction (yet)

scaffold_divergence_log.model_value: asymmetric_opening_sequence — Party A has set a limiting frame ('weekday overnights should stay limited') before Party B has had equal space to articulate interests. Party B is now structurally positioned to either defend against the limitation or accept Party A's agenda rather than surface their own needs.

position_collision_with_trust_undertow — Both parties are staking territory (Party A: control through child welfare, Party B: status through anti-gatekeeping) without yet engaging each other's actual interests. Underlying trust issue is present (Party B's 'used to block' language) but not yet dominant. Dynamic is workable if mediator can reframe toward problem-solving before positions harden further.

convergent_but_asymmetric_trust — both parties have moved from position collision to shared problem identification, but Party B still carries concern that logistics will be weaponized and Party A has not yet acknowledged Party B's equal status claim. Dynamic is workable and ready for option work IF options address both logistical constraints AND relational asymmetry (Party B as equal co-designer, not provisional parent).

notes: Turn 1 represents session initiation — no party input has occurred yet
Both parties are at baseline state awaiting process structure
No behavioral signals exist to assess emotional state or relational dynamics
Risk assessment is necessarily provisional pending party participation
Party A's language is notably conclusory ('should stay limited') rather than purely interest-stating — this is positioning, not just concern-sharing
The child-centered framing ('the child needs consistency') may be genuine but also serves to pre-legitimize Party A's preferred outcome
Party B's silence is structural, not necessarily indicative of disengagement — they simply haven't been invited yet

The relational risk here is not hostility but agenda capture — if Party B responds to Party A's specific claims rather than articulating their own interests, the mediation begins on asymmetric footing

Party B's protest framing is developmentally normal at this stage — they entered after Party A set a limiting frame and are establishing that they won't accept secondary status. This is assertion, not escalation.
Party A has not yet reacted to Party B's pushback — their silence may indicate either receptiveness to hearing more or strategic waiting. Cannot assess which yet.
Both parties are still engaging substantively with the issue rather than attacking each other — this is a healthy collision that can be redirected toward interests.
The perception agent's note about Party A's child welfare framing is validated — no personal interests have been voiced yet, which will become a problem if not addressed.
Trust erosion signal from Party B ('used to block') is early-stage — it's a perception of pattern, not an accusation of specific incident. Mediator should note but not amplify at this turn.
T6 represents significant process movement — both parties naming same three constraints is genuine shared reality
Party A's 'some openness' is first affirmative flexibility signal and must be anchored/validated to prevent regression
Party B's acceptance of trial is conditional on it not being indefinite — this is legitimate boundary, not obstruction
Neither party has yet brought child's actual voice/experience into room — remains abstract 'child needs' vs 'parental rights' frame
Perception agent note about Party B's equal legitimacy need is critical — logistical options will fail if they don't address this structural concern

party_b.current_emotional_state: guardedly_engaged — accepts process structure but vigilant for signs trial becomes indefinite gatekeeping

emotional_arc.assessment: guardedly_engaged — accepts process structure but vigilant for signs trial becomes indefinite gatekeeping

accumulated_interests.interest: Unknown — Party B has not yet spoken
Likely: meaningful parenting time during school week
Likely: being seen as capable parent
Equal parental status recognition (core identity need, not just time allocation)
Meaningful involvement in school-week parenting routines (homework, morning preparation, not just recreation)
Having logistics treated as problems to solve rather than disqualifiers
Not being relegated to 'weekend entertainment parent' role
Equal parental legitimacy recognition (core identity need)
Meaningful school-week involvement in child's routines (homework, preparation, not just recreation)
Trial structure that has clear path to normalization, not permanent probation
Being treated as co-problem-solver on logistics, not as petitioner proving worthiness
Having trial success criteria be achievable and fair, not designed to confirm existing limitations

risk_signal_history.signal: Structural disadvantage — entering conversation after limiting frame is set
Risk of defensive posture rather than interest articulation
Protest framing ('I do not want to be treated...') is reactive — has not yet articulated independent vision of desired arrangement
Accusatory edge ('used to block time') signals underlying trust erosion but not yet at unsafe level
Underlying concern that 'phased trial' becomes permanent secondary status unless path to meaningful time is made explicit
Status anxiety may resurface if logistics discussion treats Party B as accommodation rather than equal parent

relational_posture_progression.posture: assertive_protest — pushing back against limitation frame but has not yet shifted to affirmative interest articulation
conditionally_accepting — agrees to trial framework but needs assurance it leads somewhere real
